methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.5B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Helion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series F), post-money $5.4B

data.narrative:
Climeworks is a Series F climate and energy technology company that has raised over $1.0 billion to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (Lnza), with primary focus on three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers: Helion (Series F, $5.4B), TAE Technologies (Series G, $6.0B), and Form Energy (Series G, $3.5B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using $1.0B total raised as a proxy base value and applying a typical 1.3x step-up multiplier for Series F advancement, implies a valuation of $1.3B. The peer-median method yields $5.4B, reflecting the median valuation across the three selected comparables, which range from $3.5B to $6.0B. These methods reflect materially different assumptions about the company's relative stage maturity and peer positioning within the climate-tech segment.

The two methods triangulate to a range of $1.1B (low) to $6.2B (high), with a median of $3.4B. This wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty regarding appropriate peer weighting and stage-to-stage valuation progression in the frontier-energy sector. The peer-median approach anchors to observable market values for later-stage capital raises, while the stage-progression method is more conservative. Key uncertainties include the relevance of each peer's technology and commercialization trajectory to Climeworks' direct air capture model.
